req.title: Approve leave request — variant 3
req.statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for Leave Request Approval/Rejection Requirements

```python
"""
Tests for Leave Request Manager Action Requirements:
1. Manager must approve or reject leave requests within 5 business days
2. Unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin after 5 business days
3. Actions must emit webhook events for external consumers
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, date
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call, ANY
from freezegun import freeze_time
import json

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models / service imports (adjust paths to match your project layout)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from leave.models import LeaveRequest, LeaveStatus, EscalationRecord
from leave.services import LeaveRequestService, BusinessDayCalculator
from leave.escalation import EscalationService
from leave.webhooks import WebhookDispatcher, WebhookEvent


# ===========================================================================
# FIXTURES
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.fixture
def mock_webhook_dispatcher():
    return MagicMock(spec=WebhookDispatcher)


@pytest.fixture
def mock_escalation_service():
    return MagicMock(spec=EscalationService)


@pytest.fixture
def mock_leave_repo():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_notification_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def leave_service(
    mock_leave_repo,
    mock_webhook_dispatcher,
    mock_escalation_service,
    mock_notification_service,
):
    return LeaveRequestService(
        leave_repo=mock_leave_repo,
        webhook_dispatcher=mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        escalation_service=mock_escalation_service,
        notification_service=mock_notification_service,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def pending_leave_request():
    """A leave request assigned to a manager and awaiting action."""
    return LeaveRequest(
        id="LR-001",
        employee_id="EMP-100",
        manager_id="MGR-200",
        start_date=date(2024, 6, 10),
        end_date=date(2024, 6, 14),
        reason="Annual leave",
        status=LeaveStatus.PENDING,
        submitted_at=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0, 0),  # Saturday excluded later
        assigned_to_manager_at=datetime(2024, 6, 3, 9, 0, 0),  # Monday
    )


@pytest.fixture
def approved_leave_request(pending_leave_request):
    req = pending_leave_request
    req.status = LeaveStatus.APPROVED
    return req


@pytest.fixture
def rejected_leave_request(pending_leave_request):
    req = pending_leave_request
    req.status = LeaveStatus.REJECTED
    return req


# ===========================================================================
# 1. MANAGER APPROVAL TESTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestManagerApproval:
    """Manager approves a leave request."""

    def test_approve_sets_status_to_approved(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        result = leave_service.approve(
            leave_id="LR-001",
            manager_id="MGR-200",
            comment="Approved – enjoy your break.",
        )

        assert result.status == LeaveStatus.APPROVED

    def test_approve_records_actioned_by_and_timestamp(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        with freeze_time("2024-06-05 10:30:00"):
            result = leave_service.approve(
                leave_id="LR-001",
                manager_id="MGR-200",
            )

        assert result.actioned_by == "MGR-200"
        assert result.actioned_at == datetime(2024, 6, 5, 10, 30, 0)

    def test_approve_persists_updated_request(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        leave_service.approve(leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200")

        mock_leave_repo.save.assert_called_once()
        saved_request = mock_leave_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved_request.status == LeaveStatus.APPROVED

    def test_approve_stores_optional_comment(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request
        comment = "All good, approved."

        result = leave_service.approve(
            leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200", comment=comment
        )

        assert result.manager_comment == comment

    def test_approve_raises_when_request_not_found(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(LeaveRequest.DoesNotExist):
            leave_service.approve(leave_id="NONEXISTENT", manager_id="MGR-200")

    def test_approve_raises_when_wrong_manager(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError, match="not assigned"):
            leave_service.approve(leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-999")

    def test_approve_raises_when_already_actioned(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, approved_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = approved_leave_request

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="already actioned"):
            leave_service.approve(leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200")

    def test_approve_raises_when_request_escalated(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        pending_leave_request.status = LeaveStatus.ESCALATED
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="escalated"):
            leave_service.approve(leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200")


# ===========================================================================
# 2. MANAGER REJECTION TESTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestManagerRejection:
    """Manager rejects a leave request."""

    def test_reject_sets_status_to_rejected(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        result = leave_service.reject(
            leave_id="LR-001",
            manager_id="MGR-200",
            reason="Insufficient cover available.",
        )

        assert result.status == LeaveStatus.REJECTED

    def test_reject_requires_reason(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="reason is required"):
            leave_service.reject(leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200", reason="")

    def test_reject_records_actioned_by_and_timestamp(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        with freeze_time("2024-06-04 14:00:00"):
            result = leave_service.reject(
                leave_id="LR-001",
                manager_id="MGR-200",
                reason="Operational requirements.",
            )

        assert result.actioned_by == "MGR-200"
        assert result.actioned_at == datetime(2024, 6, 4, 14, 0, 0)

    def test_reject_persists_updated_request(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        leave_service.reject(
            leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200", reason="No cover."
        )

        mock_leave_repo.save.assert_called_once()
        saved = mock_leave_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved.status == LeaveStatus.REJECTED

    def test_reject_raises_when_wrong_manager(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError, match="not assigned"):
            leave_service.reject(
                leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-WRONG", reason="reason"
            )

    def test_reject_raises_when_already_actioned(
        self, leave_service, mock_leave_repo, rejected_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = rejected_leave_request

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="already actioned"):
            leave_service.reject(
                leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200", reason="reason"
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 3. BUSINESS DAY CALCULATION TESTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestBusinessDayCalculator:
    """Unit tests for the 5-business-day window logic."""

    @pytest.fixture
    def calc(self):
        return BusinessDayCalculator()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # elapsed business days
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("assigned_at, current_dt, expected_days", [
        # Assigned Monday, checked same Monday (0 full business days elapsed)
        (datetime(2024, 6, 3, 9, 0), datetime(2024, 6, 3, 17, 0), 0),
        # Assigned Monday, checked Tuesday
        (datetime(2024, 6, 3, 9, 0), datetime(2024, 6, 4, 9, 0), 1),
        # Assigned Monday, checked Friday (4 elapsed)
        (datetime(2024, 6, 3, 9, 0), datetime(2024, 6, 7, 9, 0), 4),
        # Assigned Monday, checked next Monday (5 elapsed – boundary)
        (datetime(2024, 6, 3, 9, 0), datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 0), 5),
        # Assigned Friday, weekend skipped, checked Monday (1 elapsed)
        (datetime(2024, 6, 7, 9, 0), datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 0), 1),
        # Assigned Thursday, checked following Tuesday spans weekend (3 elapsed)
        (datetime(2024, 6, 6, 9, 0), datetime(2024, 6, 11, 9, 0), 3),
    ])
    def test_elapsed_business_days(
        self, calc, assigned_at, current_dt, expected_days
    ):
        assert calc.business_days_elapsed(assigned_at, current_dt) == expected_days

    def test_public_holiday_excluded_from_business_days(self, calc):
        """25 Dec (Wednesday) should not count as a business day."""
        assigned_at = datetime(2024, 12, 23, 9, 0)  # Monday
        checked_at = datetime(2024, 12, 26, 9, 0)   # Thursday (Wed = holiday)
        # Mon->Tue = 1, Wed skipped, Thu = 2
        assert calc.business_days_elapsed(assigned_at, checked_at) == 2

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # deadline helpers
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_deadline_is_five_business_days_from_assignment(self, calc):
        assigned_at = datetime(2024, 6, 3, 9, 0)  # Monday
        deadline = calc.get_deadline(assigned_at)
        assert deadline.date() == date(2024, 6, 10)  # following Monday

    def test_deadline_skips_weekend(self, calc):
        assigned_at = datetime(2024, 6, 5, 9, 0)  # Wednesday
        deadline = calc.get_deadline(assigned_at)
        # Wed+5 business = Wed, Thu, Fri, Mon, Tue → 11 June (Tue)
        assert deadline.date() == date(2024, 6, 11)

    def test_is_within_deadline_returns_true_before_five_days(self, calc):
        assigned_at = datetime(2024, 6, 3, 9, 0)
        with freeze_time("2024-06-07 08:59:59"):  # Friday – day 4
            assert calc.is_within_deadline(assigned_at) is True

    def test_is_within_deadline_returns_false_at_exactly_five_days(self, calc):
        assigned_at = datetime(2024, 6, 3, 9, 0)
        with freeze_time("2024-06-10 09:00:00"):  # Monday = day 5 boundary
            assert calc.is_within_deadline(assigned_at) is False

    def test_is_within_deadline_returns_false_after_five_days(self, calc):
        assigned_at = datetime(2024, 6, 3, 9, 0)
        with freeze_time("2024-06-11 09:00:00"):  # Tuesday = day 6
            assert calc.is_within_deadline(assigned_at) is False


# ===========================================================================
# 4. AUTO-ESCALATION TESTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestAutoEscalation:
    """Unactioned requests escalate to hr-admin after 5 business days."""

    @pytest.fixture
    def escalation_service(
        self,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        mock_notification_service,
    ):
        return EscalationService(
            leave_repo=mock_leave_repo,
            webhook_dispatcher=mock_webhook_dispatcher,
            notification_service=mock_notification_service,
            business_day_calculator=BusinessDayCalculator(),
        )

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # identification of overdue requests
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_finds_pending_requests_assigned_over_five_business_days_ago(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo
    ):
        overdue_request = LeaveRequest(
            id="LR-002",
            status=LeaveStatus.PENDING,
            assigned_to_manager_at=datetime(2024, 6, 3, 9, 0),
        )
        mock_leave_repo.get_pending_assigned_before.return_value = [overdue_request]

        with freeze_time("2024-06-10 09:00:00"):
            overdue = escalation_service.identify_overdue_requests()

        assert overdue_request in overdue

    def test_does_not_include_requests_within_deadline(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo
    ):
        timely_request = LeaveRequest(
            id="LR-003",
            status=LeaveStatus.PENDING,
            assigned_to_manager_at=datetime(2024, 6, 7, 9, 0),  # Friday
        )
        mock_leave_repo.get_pending_assigned_before.return_value = []

        with freeze_time("2024-06-10 09:00:00"):  # Monday – only 1 business day later
            overdue = escalation_service.identify_overdue_requests()

        assert timely_request not in overdue

    def test_does_not_escalate_already_escalated_requests(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo
    ):
        already_escalated = LeaveRequest(
            id="LR-004",
            status=LeaveStatus.ESCALATED,
            assigned_to_manager_at=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0),
        )
        mock_leave_repo.get_pending_assigned_before.return_value = []

        with freeze_time("2024-06-10 09:00:00"):
            overdue = escalation_service.identify_overdue_requests()

        assert already_escalated not in overdue

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # escalation action
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_escalate_sets_status_to_escalated(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        result = escalation_service.escalate(pending_leave_request)

        assert result.status == LeaveStatus.ESCALATED

    def test_escalate_assigns_to_hr_admin(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        result = escalation_service.escalate(pending_leave_request)

        assert result.assigned_to == "hr-admin"

    def test_escalate_records_escalation_timestamp(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        with freeze_time("2024-06-10 09:05:00"):
            result = escalation_service.escalate(pending_leave_request)

        assert result.escalated_at == datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 5, 0)

    def test_escalate_records_original_manager(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        result = escalation_service.escalate(pending_leave_request)

        assert result.original_manager_id == pending_leave_request.manager_id

    def test_escalate_creates_escalation_audit_record(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        escalation_service.escalate(pending_leave_request)

        mock_leave_repo.save_escalation_record.assert_called_once()
        record: EscalationRecord = mock_leave_repo.save_escalation_record.call_args[0][0]
        assert record.leave_request_id == "LR-001"
        assert record.escalated_to == "hr-admin"

    def test_escalate_persists_updated_request(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo, pending_leave_request
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        escalation_service.escalate(pending_leave_request)

        mock_leave_repo.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_escalate_notifies_hr_admin(
        self,
        escalation_service,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_notification_service,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        escalation_service.escalate(pending_leave_request)

        mock_notification_service.notify_hr_admin.assert_called_once_with(
            leave_request=pending_leave_request,
            reason="manager_inaction",
        )

    def test_escalate_notifies_original_manager(
        self,
        escalation_service,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_notification_service,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        escalation_service.escalate(pending_leave_request)

        mock_notification_service.notify_manager.assert_called_once_with(
            manager_id="MGR-200",
            leave_request=pending_leave_request,
            reason="escalated_due_to_inaction",
        )

    def test_escalate_notifies_employee(
        self,
        escalation_service,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_notification_service,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        escalation_service.escalate(pending_leave_request)

        mock_notification_service.notify_employee.assert_called_once_with(
            employee_id=pending_leave_request.employee_id,
            leave_request=pending_leave_request,
            reason="escalated_to_hr",
        )

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # batch escalation job
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_run_escalation_job_processes_all_overdue_requests(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo
    ):
        overdue = [
            LeaveRequest(
                id=f"LR-{i}",
                status=LeaveStatus.PENDING,
                manager_id="MGR-200",
                employee_id="EMP-100",
                assigned_to_manager_at=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0),
            )
            for i in range(3)
        ]
        mock_leave_repo.get_pending_assigned_before.return_value = overdue

        with freeze_time("2024-06-10 09:00:00"):
            report = escalation_service.run_escalation_job()

        assert report.escalated_count == 3
        assert mock_leave_repo.save.call_count == 3

    def test_run_escalation_job_returns_zero_when_nothing_overdue(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_pending_assigned_before.return_value = []

        with freeze_time("2024-06-10 09:00:00"):
            report = escalation_service.run_escalation_job()

        assert report.escalated_count == 0

    def test_run_escalation_job_continues_on_single_failure(
        self, escalation_service, mock_leave_repo
    ):
        overdue = [
            LeaveRequest(
                id="LR-FAIL",
                status=LeaveStatus.PENDING,
                manager_id="MGR-200",
                employee_id="EMP-100",
                assigned_to_manager_at=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0),
            ),
            LeaveRequest(
                id="LR-OK",
                status=LeaveStatus.PENDING,
                manager_id="MGR-200",
                employee_id="EMP-101",
                assigned_to_manager_at=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0),
            ),
        ]
        mock_leave_repo.get_pending_assigned_before.return_value = overdue
        mock_leave_repo.save.side_effect = [Exception("DB error"), None]

        with freeze_time("2024-06-10 09:00:00"):
            report = escalation_service.run_escalation_job()

        assert report.escalated_count == 1
        assert report.failed_count == 1
        assert "LR-FAIL" in report.failed_ids


# ===========================================================================
# 5. WEBHOOK EMISSION TESTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestWebhookEmission:
    """Every manager action and escalation must emit a webhook event."""

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # approval webhook
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_approve_emits_webhook_event(
        self,
        leave_service,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        leave_service.approve(leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200")

        mock_webhook_dispatcher.dispatch.assert_called_once()

    def test_approve_webhook_has_correct_event_type(
        self,
        leave_service,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        leave_service.approve(leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200")

        event: WebhookEvent = mock_webhook_dispatcher.dispatch.call_args[0][0]
        assert event.event_type == "leave_request.approved"

    def test_approve_webhook_payload_contains_required_fields(
        self,
        leave_service,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        with freeze_time("2024-06-05 10:00:00"):
            leave_service.approve(leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200")

        event: WebhookEvent = mock_webhook_dispatcher.dispatch.call_args[0][0]
        payload = event.payload

        assert payload["leave_request_id"] == "LR-001"
        assert payload["employee_id"] == "EMP-100"
        assert payload["actioned_by"] == "MGR-200"
        assert payload["new_status"] == "approved"
        assert "actioned_at" in payload

    def test_approve_webhook_emitted_after_persist(
        self,
        leave_service,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        """Webhook must only fire after the DB write succeeds."""
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request
        call_order = []
        mock_leave_repo.save.side_effect = lambda _: call_order.append("save")
        mock_webhook_dispatcher.dispatch.side_effect = lambda _: call_order.append(
            "webhook"
        )

        leave_service.approve(leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200")

        assert call_order == ["save", "webhook"]

    def test_approve_does_not_emit_webhook_on_db_failure(
        self,
        leave_service,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request
        mock_leave_repo.save.side_effect = Exception("DB unavailable")

        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            leave_service.approve(leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200")

        mock_webhook_dispatcher.dispatch.assert_not_called()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # rejection webhook
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_reject_emits_webhook_event(
        self,
        leave_service,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        leave_service.reject(
            leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200", reason="No cover."
        )

        mock_webhook_dispatcher.dispatch.assert_called_once()

    def test_reject_webhook_has_correct_event_type(
        self,
        leave_service,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        leave_service.reject(
            leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200", reason="No cover."
        )

        event: WebhookEvent = mock_webhook_dispatcher.dispatch.call_args[0][0]
        assert event.event_type == "leave_request.rejected"

    def test_reject_webhook_payload_contains_rejection_reason(
        self,
        leave_service,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        leave_service.reject(
            leave_id="LR-001", manager_id="MGR-200", reason="Operational needs."
        )

        event: WebhookEvent = mock_webhook_dispatcher.dispatch.call_args[0][0]
        assert event.payload["rejection_reason"] == "Operational needs."

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # escalation webhook
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_escalation_emits_webhook_event(
        self,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        mock_notification_service,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        escalation_service = EscalationService(
            leave_repo=mock_leave_repo,
            webhook_dispatcher=mock_webhook_dispatcher,
            notification_service=mock_notification_service,
            business_day_calculator=BusinessDayCalculator(),
        )
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        escalation_service.escalate(pending_leave_request)

        mock_webhook_dispatcher.dispatch.assert_called_once()

    def test_escalation_webhook_has_correct_event_type(
        self,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        mock_notification_service,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        escalation_service = EscalationService(
            leave_repo=mock_leave_repo,
            webhook_dispatcher=mock_webhook_dispatcher,
            notification_service=mock_notification_service,
            business_day_calculator=BusinessDayCalculator(),
        )
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave_request

        escalation_service.escalate(pending_leave_request)

        event: WebhookEvent = mock_webhook_dispatcher.dispatch.call_args[0][0]
        assert event.event_type == "leave_request.escalated"

    def test_escalation_webhook_payload_contains_required_fields(
        self,
        mock_leave_repo,
        mock_webhook_dispatcher,
        mock_notification_service,
        pending_leave_request,
    ):
        escalation_service = EscalationService(
            leave_repo=mock_leave_repo,
            webhook_dispatcher=mock_webhook_dispatcher,
            notification_service=mock_notification_service,
            business_day_calculator=BusinessDayCalculator(),
        )
        mock_leave_repo.get_by_id.return_value = pending_leave
